name: Cisco Monitoring
description: Cisco-specific monitoring apps: CPU/memory, environmental health (PSU, fans, temps), device health summary, and NTP peer status.
author: MonCTL
changelog: v1.0.0 - Bundles cisco_cpu_memory 1.2.0, cisco_env_health 1.1.0, cisco_health_status 1.0.2, cisco_ntp_status 1.3.1. Requires the built-in SNMP connector.

apps.name: cisco_cpu_memory
apps.description: Cisco CPU utilization and memory usage via SNMP (IOS, IOS-XE, IOS-XR, NX-OS)

versions.source_code:
"""Cisco CPU + Memory performance app.

Collects CPU utilization and memory usage from Cisco devices via SNMP.
Supports IOS, IOS-XE, IOS-XR, and NX-OS through multi-MIB probing with
graceful fallback.

Target table: performance
Each CPU and memory pool becomes a separate component.

Connector binding required:
    alias "snmp" → SNMP connector with credential providing auth parameters

Caching:
    Entity physical names (ENTITY-MIB entPhysicalName) are cached for 24 hours
    via PollContext.cache to avoid redundant SNMP GETs on every poll cycle.
    Cache key: "entity_names" — dict mapping entPhysicalIndex (str) → name (str).

Component types produced:
    CPU     — one component per physical/virtual CPU
    Memory  — one component per memory pool

Metrics per CPU component:
    utilization_1min_pct   — 1-minute average CPU utilization (%)
    utilization_5min_pct   — 5-minute average CPU utilization (%)

Metrics per memory component:
    used_bytes     — memory currently in use
    free_bytes     — memory currently free
    total_bytes    — used + free
    used_pct       — utilization percentage

v1.2.0: Collapse the CPU and enhanced-memory walks into a single
        snmp.walk_multi() correlated GETBULK stream. The legacy
        ciscoMemoryPoolTable fallback walk still runs sequentially because
        it is conditional (only when cempMemPoolTable returns zero rows).
        Requires SNMP connector ≥ 1.4.2.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time

from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult
from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# ── OID constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# CISCO-PROCESS-MIB: cpmCPUTotalTable
_CPM_CPU_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1"
_CPM_CPU_PHYSICAL_INDEX = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.2"   # → entPhysicalIndex
_CPM_CPU_1MIN           = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.7"   # cpmCPUTotal1minRev
_CPM_CPU_5MIN           = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.8"   # cpmCPUTotal5minRev

# CISCO-ENHANCED-MEMPOOL-MIB: cempMemPoolTable (double-indexed)
_CEMP_TABLE      = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1"
_CEMP_NAME       = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1.3"    # cempMemPoolName
_CEMP_HC_USED    = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1.18"   # cempMemPoolHCUsed (Counter64)
_CEMP_HC_FREE    = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1.20"   # cempMemPoolHCFree (Counter64)
_CEMP_USED       = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1.7"    # cempMemPoolUsed (Gauge32)
_CEMP_FREE       = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.221.1.1.1.1.8"    # cempMemPoolFree (Gauge32)

# CISCO-MEMORY-POOL-MIB: ciscoMemoryPoolTable (legacy fallback)
_CMP_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1"
_CMP_NAME  = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.2"    # ciscoMemoryPoolName
_CMP_USED  = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.5"    # ciscoMemoryPoolUsed
_CMP_FREE  = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.6"    # ciscoMemoryPoolFree

# ENTITY-MIB: entPhysicalName
_ENT_PHYSICAL_NAME = "1.3.6.1.2.1.47.1.1.1.1.7"

# Cache settings
_ENTITY_NAMES_CACHE_KEY = "entity_names"
_ENTITY_NAMES_TTL = 86400  # 24 hours


def _safe_int(val, default: int = 0) -> int:
    """Convert SNMP value string to int, returning default for non-numeric."""
    if val is None:
        return default
    try:
        return int(val)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _make_component_name(name: str) -> str:
    """Sanitize entity name into a short, clean component name.

    "0/RP0-Virtual processor for RP XR" → "rp0_xr"
    "0/RP0-Virtual processor for admin" → "rp0_admin"
    Falls back to "cpu_<index>" if no meaningful name.
    """
    lower = name.lower()

    # IOS-XR virtual processor naming
    if "virtual processor" in lower:
        slot = ""
        if "/" in name:
            parts = name.split("-", 1)
            slot_raw = parts[0].strip()
            slot = slot_raw.split("/")[-1].lower() if "/" in slot_raw else slot_raw.lower()
        if "for " in lower:
            qualifier = lower.split("for ")[-1].strip()
            qualifier = qualifier.replace(" ", "_").replace("rp_", "")
            return f"{slot}_{qualifier}" if slot else qualifier
        return slot or "cpu"

    # IOS/IOS-XE/NX-OS: module names like "module 0/RSP0/CPU0"
    if "module" in lower or "cpu" in lower or "rsp" in lower:
        cleaned = name.replace("module ", "").replace("Module ", "")
        cleaned = cleaned.strip().replace("/", "_").replace(" ", "_").lower()
        return cleaned

    # Generic fallback: slugify
    return name.strip().replace("/", "_").replace(" ", "_").replace("-", "_").lower()


def _make_memory_component_name(entity_name: str, pool_name: str) -> str:
    """Build a memory component name from entity + pool.

    entity_name: resolved entPhysicalName (e.g., "0/RP0-Virtual processor for RP XR")
    pool_name: cempMemPoolName (e.g., "processor", "reserved", "image")

    Result: "rp0_xr_processor", "rp0_admin_processor", etc.
    For legacy MIB (no entity): just "processor", "io", etc.
    """
    if entity_name:
        entity_short = _make_component_name(entity_name)
        return f"{entity_short}_{pool_name.lower()}"
    return pool_name.lower().replace(" ", "_")


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """Cisco CPU + Memory poller — uses the SNMP connector."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        start = time.time()

        logger.debug("poll: entering; host=%s device_id=%s node=%s",
                     context.device_host, context.job.device_id, context.node_id)
        logger.debug("poll: connectors=%s parameters=%r",
                     list(context.connectors.keys()), context.parameters)

        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            logger.debug("poll: no snmp connector bound; returning config error")
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="error",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=(
                    "No SNMP connector bound (expected alias 'snmp'). "
                    "Assign an SNMP connector with alias 'snmp' to this app assignment."
                ),
                error_category="config",
                execution_time_ms=0,
            )

        logger.debug("poll: snmp connector=%s cache=%s",
                     type(snmp).__name__, type(context.cache).__name__ if context.cache else None)

        try:
            metrics = []

            # ── Phases 1+2: CPU and enhanced memory in one correlated walk ──
            logger.debug("phase=cpu+cemp: walk_multi over %s and %s",
                         _CPM_CPU_TABLE, _CEMP_TABLE)
            t0 = time.time()
            per_oid = await snmp.walk_multi([_CPM_CPU_TABLE, _CEMP_TABLE])
            cpu_rows = per_oid.get(_CPM_CPU_TABLE, [])
            cemp_rows = per_oid.get(_CEMP_TABLE, [])
            logger.debug("phase=cpu+cemp: walk_multi cpu=%d rows cemp=%d rows in %d ms",
                         len(cpu_rows), len(cemp_rows), int((time.time() - t0) * 1000))

            if cpu_rows:
                cpu_metrics = await self._collect_cpu(snmp, context, cpu_rows)
                logger.debug("phase=cpu: produced %d metrics", len(cpu_metrics))
                metrics.extend(cpu_metrics)
            else:
                logger.warning("cisco_cpu_memory: cpmCPUTotalTable walk returned no data")

            if cemp_rows:
                mem_metrics = await self._collect_memory_enhanced(snmp, context, cemp_rows)
                logger.debug("phase=mem: enhanced produced %d metrics", len(mem_metrics))
                metrics.extend(mem_metrics)
            else:
                # Legacy fallback — only runs when the modern MIB is absent.
                logger.debug("phase=mem: enhanced empty, falling back to ciscoMemoryPoolTable %s",
                             _CMP_TABLE)
                t0 = time.time()
                cmp_rows = await snmp.walk(_CMP_TABLE)
                logger.debug("phase=mem: cmp walk returned %d rows in %d ms",
                             len(cmp_rows or []), int((time.time() - t0) * 1000))
                if cmp_rows:
                    mem_metrics = self._collect_memory_legacy(cmp_rows)
                    logger.debug("phase=mem: legacy produced %d metrics", len(mem_metrics))
                    metrics.extend(mem_metrics)
                else:
                    logger.warning("cisco_cpu_memory: no memory MIB data available")

            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            logger.debug("poll: finished phases; total metrics=%d exec=%d ms",
                         len(metrics), execution_ms)

            if not metrics:
                logger.debug("poll: zero metrics — returning device-error result")
                return PollResult(
                    job_id=context.job.job_id,
                    device_id=context.job.device_id,
                    collector_node=context.node_id,
                    timestamp=time.time(),
                    metrics=[],
                    config_data=None,
                    status="error",
                    reachable=True,
                    error_message=(
                        "No CPU/memory MIB data available — device may not "
                        "support CISCO-PROCESS-MIB / CISCO-MEMORY-POOL-MIB "
                        "(walks returned no rows)."
                    ),
                    error_category="device",
                    execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
                )
            for m in metrics:
                logger.debug("metric: component=%s type=%s names=%s values=%s",
                             m.get("component"), m.get("component_type"),
                             m.get("metric_names"), m.get("metric_values"))
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=metrics,
                config_data=None,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            logger.debug("poll: exception %s: %s (exec=%d ms)",
                         type(exc).__name__, exc, execution_ms, exc_info=True)
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
                error_category="device",
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

    # ── Entity name resolution with caching ───────────────────────────────

    async def _resolve_entity_names(
        self,
        snmp,
        context: PollContext,
        needed_indices: set[str],
    ) -> dict[int, str]:
        """Resolve entPhysicalIndex values to entPhysicalName strings.

        Uses PollContext.cache to store results for 24 hours.
        Only fetches indices not already present in the cache.
        """
        logger.debug("entity_names: needed=%s", sorted(needed_indices))
        if not needed_indices:
            return {}

        cached: dict[str, str] = {}
        if context.cache is not None:
            try:
                cached_data = await context.cache.get(_ENTITY_NAMES_CACHE_KEY)
                if cached_data is not None:
                    cached = cached_data
                logger.debug("entity_names: cache hit=%d entries", len(cached))
            except Exception as exc:
                logger.debug("cisco_cpu_memory: cache read failed: %s", exc)
        else:
            logger.debug("entity_names: no cache available (debug run)")

        missing = needed_indices - set(cached.keys())
        logger.debug("entity_names: missing=%s", sorted(missing))

        if not missing:
            return {int(k): v for k, v in cached.items() if k in needed_indices}

        fetched: dict[str, str] = {}
        try:
            oids_to_get = [f"{_ENT_PHYSICAL_NAME}.{idx}" for idx in missing]
            logger.debug("entity_names: SNMP GET %d OIDs", len(oids_to_get))
            t0 = time.time()
            result = await snmp.get(oids_to_get)
            logger.debug("entity_names: got %d responses in %d ms",
                         len(result), int((time.time() - t0) * 1000))
            for oid, val in result.items():
                idx_str = oid.rsplit(".", 1)[-1]
                val_str = str(val)
                if "no such" not in val_str.lower():
                    fetched[idx_str] = val_str
                    logger.debug("entity_names: resolved idx=%s → %r", idx_str, val_str)
                else:
                    logger.debug("entity_names: idx=%s unresolved (%s)", idx_str, val_str)
        except Exception as exc:
            logger.debug("cisco_cpu_memory: entity name SNMP lookup failed: %s", exc)

        if fetched:
            merged = {**cached, **fetched}
            if context.cache is not None:
                try:
                    await context.cache.set(
                        _ENTITY_NAMES_CACHE_KEY, merged, ttl=_ENTITY_NAMES_TTL,
                    )
                    logger.debug("entity_names: cache updated, total=%d", len(merged))
                except Exception as exc:
                    logger.debug("cisco_cpu_memory: cache write failed: %s", exc)
        else:
            merged = cached

        resolved = {int(k): v for k, v in merged.items() if k in needed_indices}
        logger.debug("entity_names: returning %d resolved: %r", len(resolved), resolved)
        return resolved

    # ── CPU collection ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    async def _collect_cpu(
        self, snmp, context: PollContext, walk_rows: list[tuple[str, str]]
    ) -> list[dict]:
        """Parse cpmCPUTotalTable walk and resolve entity names."""

        cpus: dict[str, dict] = {}
        base_parts = _CPM_CPU_TABLE.split(".")
        base_len = len(base_parts)

        logger.debug("cpu: parsing %d walk rows (base_len=%d)", len(walk_rows), base_len)
        for oid, val in walk_rows:
            parts = oid.split(".")
            if len(parts) <= base_len + 1:
                continue
            column = parts[base_len]
            cpu_index = ".".join(parts[base_len + 1:])

            if cpu_index not in cpus:
                cpus[cpu_index] = {}

            if column == "2":
                cpus[cpu_index]["phys_idx"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif column == "7":
                cpus[cpu_index]["1min"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif column == "8":
                cpus[cpu_index]["5min"] = _safe_int(val)

        logger.debug("cpu: parsed %d cpu rows: %r", len(cpus), cpus)
        if not cpus:
            return []

        phys_indices = {
            str(c["phys_idx"]) for c in cpus.values()
            if c.get("phys_idx") and c["phys_idx"] > 0
        }
        logger.debug("cpu: phys_indices to resolve: %s", sorted(phys_indices))

        entity_names = await self._resolve_entity_names(snmp, context, phys_indices)

        metrics = []
        for cpu_index, data in cpus.items():
            phys_idx = data.get("phys_idx", 0)
            entity_name = entity_names.get(phys_idx, "")
            component_name = (
                _make_component_name(entity_name) if entity_name
                else f"cpu_{cpu_index}"
            )
            logger.debug("cpu: idx=%s phys_idx=%s entity=%r component=%s 1min=%s 5min=%s",
                         cpu_index, phys_idx, entity_name, component_name,
                         data.get("1min"), data.get("5min"))

            metric_names = []
            metric_values = []

            if "1min" in data:
                metric_names.append("utilization_1min_pct")
                metric_values.append(float(data["1min"]))
            if "5min" in data:
                metric_names.append("utilization_5min_pct")
                metric_values.append(float(data["5min"]))

            if metric_names:
                metrics.append({
                    "component": component_name,
                    "component_type": "CPU",
                    "metric_names": metric_names,
                    "metric_values": metric_values,
                })
            else:
                logger.debug("cpu: idx=%s dropped (no metric values)", cpu_index)

        return metrics

    # ── Memory collection: CISCO-ENHANCED-MEMPOOL-MIB ─────────────────────

    async def _collect_memory_enhanced(
        self, snmp, context: PollContext, walk_rows: list[tuple[str, str]]
    ) -> list[dict]:
        """Parse cempMemPoolTable walk (double-indexed by entPhysIndex.poolIndex)."""

        pools: dict[str, dict] = {}
        base_parts = _CEMP_TABLE.split(".")
        base_len = len(base_parts)

        logger.debug("mem_enhanced: parsing %d walk rows", len(walk_rows))
        for oid, val in walk_rows:
            parts = oid.split(".")
            if len(parts) <= base_len + 2:
                continue
            column = parts[base_len]
            compound_index = ".".join(parts[base_len + 1:])

            if compound_index not in pools:
                pools[compound_index] = {}

            col = int(column)
            if col == 3:
                pools[compound_index]["name"] = str(val).strip('"').strip()
            elif col == 7:
                pools[compound_index]["used_32"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif col == 8:
                pools[compound_index]["free_32"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif col == 18:
                pools[compound_index]["hc_used"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif col == 20:
                pools[compound_index]["hc_free"] = _safe_int(val)

        logger.debug("mem_enhanced: parsed %d pools", len(pools))
        if not pools:
            return []

        entity_indices = {compound.split(".")[0] for compound in pools}
        logger.debug("mem_enhanced: entity indices: %s", sorted(entity_indices))

        entity_names = await self._resolve_entity_names(snmp, context, entity_indices)

        metrics = []
        for compound_index, data in pools.items():
            pool_name = data.get("name", "unknown")
            ent_idx = _safe_int(compound_index.split(".")[0])
            entity_name = entity_names.get(ent_idx, "")

            used = data.get("hc_used")
            free = data.get("hc_free")
            used_source = "hc"
            if used is None or used == 0:
                used = data.get("used_32", 0)
                used_source = "32"
            if free is None or free == 0:
                free = data.get("free_32", 0)

            logger.debug(
                "mem_enhanced: compound=%s ent=%s pool=%r used=%s free=%s (src=%s)",
                compound_index, entity_name, pool_name, used, free, used_source,
            )

            if used == 0 and free == 0:
                logger.debug("mem_enhanced: compound=%s dropped (both counters zero)", compound_index)
                continue

            total = used + free
            used_pct = round((used / total) * 100, 2) if total > 0 else 0.0

            component_name = _make_memory_component_name(entity_name, pool_name)

            metrics.append({
                "component": component_name,
                "component_type": "Memory",
                "metric_names": [
                    "used_bytes",
                    "free_bytes",
                    "total_bytes",
                    "used_pct",
                ],
                "metric_values": [
                    float(used),
                    float(free),
                    float(total),
                    used_pct,
                ],
            })

        return metrics

    # ── Memory collection: CISCO-MEMORY-POOL-MIB (legacy fallback) ────────

    def _collect_memory_legacy(self, walk_rows: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> list[dict]:
        """Parse ciscoMemoryPoolTable walk (single-indexed by pool ID)."""

        pools: dict[str, dict] = {}
        base_parts = _CMP_TABLE.split(".")
        base_len = len(base_parts)

        logger.debug("mem_legacy: parsing %d walk rows", len(walk_rows))
        for oid, val in walk_rows:
            parts = oid.split(".")
            if len(parts) <= base_len + 1:
                continue
            column = parts[base_len]
            pool_index = ".".join(parts[base_len + 1:])

            if pool_index not in pools:
                pools[pool_index] = {}

            col = int(column)
            if col == 2:
                pools[pool_index]["name"] = str(val).strip('"').strip()
            elif col == 5:
                pools[pool_index]["used"] = _safe_int(val)
            elif col == 6:
                pools[pool_index]["free"] = _safe_int(val)

        logger.debug("mem_legacy: parsed %d pools", len(pools))
        metrics = []
        for pool_index, data in pools.items():
            pool_name = data.get("name", f"pool_{pool_index}")
            used = data.get("used", 0)
            free = data.get("free", 0)

            logger.debug("mem_legacy: idx=%s pool=%r used=%s free=%s",
                         pool_index, pool_name, used, free)

            if used == 0 and free == 0:
                logger.debug("mem_legacy: idx=%s dropped (both counters zero)", pool_index)
                continue

            total = used + free
            used_pct = round((used / total) * 100, 2) if total > 0 else 0.0

            component_name = pool_name.lower().replace(" ", "_")

            metrics.append({
                "component": component_name,
                "component_type": "Memory",
                "metric_names": [
                    "used_bytes",
                    "free_bytes",
                    "total_bytes",
                    "used_pct",
                ],
                "metric_values": [
                    float(used),
                    float(free),
                    float(total),
                    used_pct,
                ],
            })

        return metrics

apps.name: cisco_env_health
apps.description: Walks CISCO-ENVMON-MIB tables (temperature, fan, PSU, voltage) and reports overall environmental health status. Writes summary and per-component detail to the config table.

versions.source_code:
"""Cisco environmental health poller — CISCO-ENVMON-MIB.

Walks temperature, fan, PSU, and voltage tables from
ciscoEnvMonMIB (1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13) and reports summary
status plus per-component detail as config_data.

Connector binding required:
    alias "snmp" → SNMP connector with credential

Writes to: config table
    env_overall_status       "normal" / "warning" / "critical"
    fan_overall_status       "normal" / "degraded" / "critical"
    psu_overall_status       "normal" / "degraded" / "critical"
    temp_overall_status      "normal" / "warning" / "critical"
    voltage_overall_status   "normal" / "warning" / "critical"
    fan_count, psu_count, temp_sensor_count  component counts
    temp_highest_celsius     highest temperature reading
    fan_details, psu_details, temp_details, voltage_details  JSON arrays

v1.1.0: Collapse the 4 sequential SNMP walks (temperature, fan, PSU, voltage)
        into a single snmp.walk_multi() correlated GETBULK stream. Requires
        SNMP connector ≥ 1.4.2. Also fixes pre-existing hygiene gaps —
        start-of-poll timing discipline, error_category on every return
        path, and logger.debug tracing.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import binascii
import json
import logging
import time

from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult
from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# CISCO-ENVMON-MIB table base OIDs
_TEMP_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.3.1"   # ciscoEnvMonTemperatureStatusTable
_FAN_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.4.1"    # ciscoEnvMonFanStatusTable
_PSU_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.5.1"    # ciscoEnvMonSupplyStatusTable
_VOLT_TABLE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.2.1"   # ciscoEnvMonVoltageStatusTable

_TABLES = [_TEMP_TABLE, _FAN_TABLE, _PSU_TABLE, _VOLT_TABLE]

# Column offsets per table
_TEMP_COLS = {"descr": "2", "value": "3", "state": "6"}
_FAN_COLS = {"descr": "2", "state": "3"}
_PSU_COLS = {"descr": "2", "state": "3", "source": "4"}
_VOLT_COLS = {"descr": "2", "value": "3", "state": "7"}

# ciscoEnvMonState enumeration
_STATE_MAP = {
    "1": "normal",
    "2": "warning",
    "3": "critical",
    "4": "shutdown",
    "5": "notPresent",
    "6": "notFunctioning",
}

_PSU_SOURCE_MAP = {
    "1": "unknown",
    "2": "ac",
    "3": "dc",
    "4": "externalPowerSupply",
    "5": "internalRedundant",
}


def _decode(value) -> str:
    val_str = str(value)
    if val_str.startswith("0x") and len(val_str) > 2:
        try:
            return binascii.unhexlify(val_str[2:]).decode("utf-8", errors="replace").rstrip("\x00")
        except (ValueError, UnicodeDecodeError):
            pass
    return val_str


def _safe_int(value, default=0) -> int:
    try:
        return int(str(value))
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _parse_table(walk_results: list, base_oid: str, columns: dict) -> dict:
    """Parse SNMP walk results into {row_index: {col_name: value}}."""
    prefix = base_oid + "."
    rows: dict[str, dict] = {}
    col_to_name = {v: k for k, v in columns.items()}

    for oid, value in walk_results:
        oid_str = str(oid)
        if not oid_str.startswith(prefix):
            continue
        suffix = oid_str[len(prefix):]  # e.g. "6.1005"
        parts = suffix.split(".", 1)
        if len(parts) != 2:
            continue
        col_id, row_idx = parts[0], parts[1]
        col_name = col_to_name.get(col_id)
        if col_name is None:
            continue
        rows.setdefault(row_idx, {})[col_name] = value
    return rows


def _subsystem_status(states: list[str], use_degraded: bool = False) -> str:
    """Compute worst-case status from a list of state strings."""
    if not states:
        return "normal"
    has_critical = any(s in ("critical", "shutdown", "notFunctioning") for s in states)
    has_warning = any(s == "warning" for s in states)
    if has_critical:
        return "critical"
    if has_warning:
        return "degraded" if use_degraded else "warning"
    return "normal"


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """Cisco environmental health poller."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        start = time.monotonic()
        host = context.device_host or context.parameters.get("host", "localhost")
        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[], config_data=None,
                status="error", reachable=False,
                error_message="No SNMP connector bound (expected alias 'snmp')",
                error_category="config",
                execution_time_ms=int((time.monotonic() - start) * 1000),
            )

        try:
            logger.debug("cisco_env_health: walk_multi over %d tables for %s",
                         len(_TABLES), host)
            per_oid = await snmp.walk_multi(_TABLES)
            rtt_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000

            temp_raw = per_oid.get(_TEMP_TABLE, [])
            fan_raw = per_oid.get(_FAN_TABLE, [])
            psu_raw = per_oid.get(_PSU_TABLE, [])
            volt_raw = per_oid.get(_VOLT_TABLE, [])
            logger.debug("  raw rows — temp=%d fan=%d psu=%d volt=%d",
                         len(temp_raw), len(fan_raw), len(psu_raw), len(volt_raw))

            # Parse tables
            temp_rows = _parse_table(temp_raw, _TEMP_TABLE, _TEMP_COLS)
            fan_rows = _parse_table(fan_raw, _FAN_TABLE, _FAN_COLS)
            psu_rows = _parse_table(psu_raw, _PSU_TABLE, _PSU_COLS)
            volt_rows = _parse_table(volt_raw, _VOLT_TABLE, _VOLT_COLS)

            # Build per-component details (exclude notPresent)
            temp_details = []
            temp_states = []
            max_temp = 0
            for idx, cols in sorted(temp_rows.items()):
                state = _STATE_MAP.get(str(_safe_int(cols.get("state"))), "unknown")
                if state == "notPresent":
                    continue
                temp_val = _safe_int(cols.get("value"))
                if temp_val > max_temp:
                    max_temp = temp_val
                temp_states.append(state)
                temp_details.append({
                    "idx": idx,
                    "descr": _decode(cols.get("descr", "")),
                    "value": temp_val,
                    "state": state,
                })

            fan_details = []
            fan_states = []
            for idx, cols in sorted(fan_rows.items()):
                state = _STATE_MAP.get(str(_safe_int(cols.get("state"))), "unknown")
                if state == "notPresent":
                    continue
                fan_states.append(state)
                fan_details.append({
                    "idx": idx,
                    "descr": _decode(cols.get("descr", "")),
                    "state": state,
                })

            psu_details = []
            psu_states = []
            for idx, cols in sorted(psu_rows.items()):
                state = _STATE_MAP.get(str(_safe_int(cols.get("state"))), "unknown")
                if state == "notPresent":
                    continue
                psu_states.append(state)
                psu_details.append({
                    "idx": idx,
                    "descr": _decode(cols.get("descr", "")),
                    "state": state,
                    "source": _PSU_SOURCE_MAP.get(
                        str(_safe_int(cols.get("source"))), "unknown"
                    ),
                })

            volt_details = []
            volt_states = []
            for idx, cols in sorted(volt_rows.items()):
                state = _STATE_MAP.get(str(_safe_int(cols.get("state"))), "unknown")
                if state == "notPresent":
                    continue
                volt_states.append(state)
                volt_details.append({
                    "idx": idx,
                    "descr": _decode(cols.get("descr", "")),
                    "value": _safe_int(cols.get("value")),
                    "state": state,
                })

            # Compute summary statuses
            temp_status = _subsystem_status(temp_states)
            fan_status = _subsystem_status(fan_states, use_degraded=True)
            psu_status = _subsystem_status(psu_states, use_degraded=True)
            volt_status = _subsystem_status(volt_states)

            # Overall = worst of all subsystems
            all_statuses = []
            if temp_states:
                all_statuses.append(temp_status)
            if fan_states:
                all_statuses.append(fan_status)
            if psu_states:
                all_statuses.append(psu_status)
            if volt_states:
                all_statuses.append(volt_status)

            if any(s == "critical" for s in all_statuses):
                env_overall = "critical"
            elif any(s in ("warning", "degraded") for s in all_statuses):
                env_overall = "warning"
            else:
                env_overall = "normal"

            logger.debug("  parsed — temp=%d fan=%d psu=%d volt=%d  overall=%s",
                         len(temp_details), len(fan_details), len(psu_details),
                         len(volt_details), env_overall)

            config_data = {
                "env_overall_status": env_overall,
                "fan_overall_status": fan_status,
                "psu_overall_status": psu_status,
                "temp_overall_status": temp_status,
                "voltage_overall_status": volt_status,
                "fan_count": str(len(fan_details)),
                "psu_count": str(len(psu_details)),
                "temp_sensor_count": str(len(temp_details)),
                "temp_highest_celsius": str(max_temp),
                "fan_details": json.dumps(fan_details),
                "psu_details": json.dumps(psu_details),
                "temp_details": json.dumps(temp_details),
                "voltage_details": json.dumps(volt_details),
            }

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[
                    {"name": "env_reachable", "value": 1.0, "labels": {"host": host}},
                ],
                config_data=config_data,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=int(rtt_ms),
                rtt_ms=rtt_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            rtt_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[
                    {"name": "env_reachable", "value": 0.0, "labels": {"host": host}},
                ],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"Cisco ENVMON query failed — {host}: {exc}",
                error_category="device",
                execution_time_ms=int(rtt_ms),
                rtt_ms=0.0,
            )

alert_definitions.name: Cisco Environment Critical
alert_definitions.description: One or more environmental sensors (fan, PSU, temperature, voltage) report critical, shutdown, or not-functioning state.

alert_definitions.name: Cisco Environment Warning
alert_definitions.description: One or more environmental sensors report warning or degraded state. Investigate sensor details.

alert_definitions.name: Cisco Fan Failure
alert_definitions.description: One or more fans are not operating normally. May indicate hardware failure or blocked airflow.

alert_definitions.name: Cisco PSU Failure
alert_definitions.description: One or more power supplies are not operating normally. Check for failed or unplugged PSU.

apps.name: cisco_health_status
apps.description: Collects CPU utilization, memory pool usage, temperature, fan state, and power supply state from Cisco devices via SNMP. Requires the built-in SNMP connector bound as alias 'snmp'.

versions.source_code:
"""Cisco Health Status — collects CPU, memory, temperature, fan, and PSU metrics.

Requires the built-in SNMP connector bound as alias 'snmp' on the assignment.
Targets: Cisco IOS, IOS-XE, NX-OS devices.

Performance metrics produced:
  Component type 'cpu':
    cpu_5sec_pct      - CPU utilization last 5 seconds
    cpu_1min_pct      - CPU utilization last 1 minute
    cpu_5min_pct      - CPU utilization last 5 minutes

  Component type 'memory':
    used_bytes        - Memory pool bytes in use
    free_bytes        - Memory pool bytes free
    utilization_pct   - Calculated usage percentage

  Component type 'temperature':
    temperature_c     - Sensor reading in degrees Celsius
    threshold_c       - Shutdown threshold

  Component type 'fan':
    state             - 1=normal, 2=warning, 3=critical, 4=shutdown, 5=notPresent, 6=notFunctioning

  Component type 'psu':
    state             - 1=normal, 2=warning, 3=critical, 4=shutdown, 5=notPresent, 6=notFunctioning

v1.0.2: Collapse 13 sequential SNMP walks (3 CPU + 3 memory + 3 temperature
        + 2 fan + 2 PSU columns) into a single snmp.walk_multi() correlated
        GETBULK stream. Requires SNMP connector ≥ 1.4.2. Also: first-line
        timing discipline, error_category on all error paths, dense
        logger.debug tracing, and drop the explicit `snmp.connect()` call
        (polling engine owns connect/close per CLAUDE.md).
"""

import logging
import time

from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller
from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


# ── CISCO-PROCESS-MIB ────────────────────────────────────
# cpmCPUTotalTable — one row per physical CPU entity
_CPU_OIDS = {
    "cpu_5sec_pct":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.6",   # cpmCPUTotal5secRev
    "cpu_1min_pct":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.7",   # cpmCPUTotal1minRev
    "cpu_5min_pct":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.109.1.1.1.1.8",   # cpmCPUTotal5minRev
}

# ── CISCO-MEMORY-POOL-MIB ────────────────────────────────
_MEM_OIDS = {
    "name":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.2",   # ciscoMemoryPoolName
    "used":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.5",   # ciscoMemoryPoolUsed
    "free":  "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.48.1.1.1.6",   # ciscoMemoryPoolFree
}

# ── CISCO-ENVMON-MIB ─────────────────────────────────────
_TEMP_OIDS = {
    "descr":     "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.3.1.2",  # ciscoEnvMonTemperatureStatusDescr
    "value":     "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.3.1.3",  # ciscoEnvMonTemperatureStatusValue
    "threshold": "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.3.1.4",  # ciscoEnvMonTemperatureThreshold
}

_FAN_OIDS = {
    "descr": "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.4.1.2",  # ciscoEnvMonFanStatusDescr
    "state": "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.4.1.3",  # ciscoEnvMonFanState
}

_PSU_OIDS = {
    "descr": "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.5.1.2",  # ciscoEnvMonSupplyStatusDescr
    "state": "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.13.1.5.1.3",  # ciscoEnvMonSupplyState
}


def _safe_int(val, default=0):
    try:
        return int(val)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _safe_float(val, default=0.0):
    try:
        return float(val)
    except (ValueError, TypeError):
        return default


def _walk_table(rows, base_oid):
    """Parse SNMP walk rows into {index: value} dict."""
    result = {}
    prefix = base_oid + "."
    for oid_str, val in rows:
        if oid_str.startswith(prefix):
            idx = oid_str[len(prefix):]
            result[idx] = str(val)
    return result


class Poller(BasePoller):
    """Cisco health poller — CPU, memory, environment sensors."""

    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        start = time.time()
        host = context.device_host

        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="error",
                reachable=False,
                error_message="No SNMP connector bound. "
                              "Bind the built-in 'snmp' connector with alias 'snmp' on this assignment.",
                error_category="config",
                execution_time_ms=int((time.time() - start) * 1000),
            )

        try:
            # One correlated GETBULK stream over all 13 columns we care about.
            all_oids = {
                **{f"cpu_{k}": v for k, v in _CPU_OIDS.items()},
                **{f"mem_{k}": v for k, v in _MEM_OIDS.items()},
                **{f"temp_{k}": v for k, v in _TEMP_OIDS.items()},
                **{f"fan_{k}": v for k, v in _FAN_OIDS.items()},
                **{f"psu_{k}": v for k, v in _PSU_OIDS.items()},
            }
            logger.debug("cisco_health_status: walk_multi over %d columns for %s",
                         len(all_oids), host)
            per_oid = await snmp.walk_multi(list(all_oids.values()))

            def _tbl(base_oid: str) -> dict:
                return _walk_table(per_oid.get(base_oid, []), base_oid)

            cpu_data = {key: _tbl(base) for key, base in _CPU_OIDS.items()}
            mem_names = _tbl(_MEM_OIDS["name"])
            mem_used = _tbl(_MEM_OIDS["used"])
            mem_free = _tbl(_MEM_OIDS["free"])
            temp_descr = _tbl(_TEMP_OIDS["descr"])
            temp_value = _tbl(_TEMP_OIDS["value"])
            temp_thresh = _tbl(_TEMP_OIDS["threshold"])
            fan_descr = _tbl(_FAN_OIDS["descr"])
            fan_state = _tbl(_FAN_OIDS["state"])
            psu_descr = _tbl(_PSU_OIDS["descr"])
            psu_state = _tbl(_PSU_OIDS["state"])
            logger.debug(
                "  rows — cpu=%d/%d/%d mem=%d/%d/%d temp=%d/%d/%d fan=%d/%d psu=%d/%d",
                len(cpu_data["cpu_5sec_pct"]), len(cpu_data["cpu_1min_pct"]), len(cpu_data["cpu_5min_pct"]),
                len(mem_names), len(mem_used), len(mem_free),
                len(temp_descr), len(temp_value), len(temp_thresh),
                len(fan_descr), len(fan_state),
                len(psu_descr), len(psu_state),
            )

            metrics = []

            # ── CPU ──
            cpu_indices = set()
            for d in cpu_data.values():
                cpu_indices.update(d.keys())

            for idx in sorted(cpu_indices):
                component = f"cpu_{idx}" if len(cpu_indices) > 1 else "cpu"
                m_names = []
                m_values = []
                for key in ("cpu_5sec_pct", "cpu_1min_pct", "cpu_5min_pct"):
                    val = cpu_data.get(key, {}).get(idx)
                    if val is not None:
                        m_names.append(key)
                        m_values.append(_safe_float(val))
                if m_names:
                    metrics.append({
                        "component": component,
                        "component_type": "cpu",
                        "metric_names": m_names,
                        "metric_values": m_values,
                    })

            # ── Memory ──
            for idx in sorted(mem_used.keys()):
                pool_name = mem_names.get(idx, f"pool_{idx}").lower().replace(" ", "_")
                used = _safe_int(mem_used.get(idx, 0))
                free = _safe_int(mem_free.get(idx, 0))
                total = used + free
                pct = (used / total * 100.0) if total > 0 else 0.0

                metrics.append({
                    "component": pool_name,
                    "component_type": "memory",
                    "metric_names": ["used_bytes", "free_bytes", "utilization_pct"],
                    "metric_values": [float(used), float(free), round(pct, 2)],
                })

            # ── Temperature ──
            for idx in sorted(temp_value.keys()):
                sensor_name = temp_descr.get(idx, f"sensor_{idx}").lower().replace(" ", "_")
                m_names = ["temperature_c"]
                m_values = [_safe_float(temp_value.get(idx, 0))]
                thresh = temp_thresh.get(idx)
                if thresh is not None:
                    m_names.append("threshold_c")
                    m_values.append(_safe_float(thresh))
                metrics.append({
                    "component": sensor_name,
                    "component_type": "temperature",
                    "metric_names": m_names,
                    "metric_values": m_values,
                })

            # ── Fans ──
            for idx in sorted(fan_state.keys()):
                fan_name = fan_descr.get(idx, f"fan_{idx}").lower().replace(" ", "_")
                metrics.append({
                    "component": fan_name,
                    "component_type": "fan",
                    "metric_names": ["state"],
                    "metric_values": [_safe_float(fan_state[idx])],
                })

            # ── Power Supplies ──
            for idx in sorted(psu_state.keys()):
                psu_name = psu_descr.get(idx, f"psu_{idx}").lower().replace(" ", "_")
                metrics.append({
                    "component": psu_name,
                    "component_type": "psu",
                    "metric_names": ["state"],
                    "metric_values": [_safe_float(psu_state[idx])],
                })

            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            logger.debug("  emitted %d component metric rows in %d ms",
                         len(metrics), execution_ms)

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=metrics,
                config_data=None,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
                error_category="device",
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

apps.name: cisco_ntp_status
apps.description: Collects NTP system status and peer information from Cisco devices via CISCO-NTP-MIB SNMP OIDs. Requires the built-in SNMP connector bound as alias 'snmp' on the assignment.

versions.source_code:
"""Cisco NTP Status - collects NTP system status and peer table via CISCO-NTP-MIB.

Requires the built-in SNMP connector bound as alias 'snmp' on the assignment.
Targets: Cisco IOS, IOS-XE, NX-OS devices.

Config keys produced:
  System:
    ntp_sync_status       - "healthy" | "warning" | "critical" | "unknown"
                            Derived from peer reachability:
                              all peers reachable     -> healthy
                              some peers unreachable  -> warning
                              no peers reachable      -> critical
                              no peers configured     -> unknown
    ntp_stratum           - Stratum level (1-16, "16" = unsynchronized)
    ntp_peer_count        - Total number of configured peers
    ntp_peers_reachable   - Number of reachable peers (reach > 0)
    ntp_peers_unreachable - Number of unreachable peers (reach == 0)
  Per-peer (flat keys: peer_0_address, peer_1_address, etc.):
    peer_N_address        - IP address of the peer
    peer_N_stratum        - Peer's stratum level
    peer_N_mode           - Peer mode (client/server/symmetric/broadcast)
    peer_N_reachable      - "yes" or "no"
    peer_N_configured     - "yes" or "no" (statically configured vs discovered)
"""

import time

from monctl_collector.polling.base import BasePoller
from monctl_collector.jobs.models import PollContext, PollResult


# -- OID constants ---------------------------------------------------------

_SYS_BASE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.168.1.1"

_SYS_OIDS = {
    "ntp_stratum": f"{_SYS_BASE}.2.0",
}

_PEER_TABLE_BASE = "1.3.6.1.4.1.9.9.168.1.2.1.1"

_PEER_OIDS = {
    "configured":  f"{_PEER_TABLE_BASE}.2",
    "address":     f"{_PEER_TABLE_BASE}.3",
    "mode":        f"{_PEER_TABLE_BASE}.8",
    "stratum":     f"{_PEER_TABLE_BASE}.9",
    # cntpPeersReach (CISCO-NTP-MIB col 21) — verified via snmpwalk on test01oh01 on 2026-04-10
    "reach":       f"{_PEER_TABLE_BASE}.21",
}

_MODE_MAP = {
    "1": "symmetric_active",
    "2": "symmetric_passive",
    "3": "client",
    "4": "server",
    "5": "broadcast",
    "6": "control",
    "7": "private",
}


class Poller(BasePoller):
    async def poll(self, context: PollContext) -> PollResult:
        host = context.device_host
        start = time.time()

        # -- Require SNMP connector ------------------------------------
        snmp = context.connectors.get("snmp")
        if snmp is None:
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message="No SNMP connector bound. Bind the built-in 'snmp' connector with alias 'snmp' on this assignment.",
                execution_time_ms=0,
                error_category="config",
            )

        try:
            await snmp.connect(host)

            # -- Collect system-level NTP status -----------------------
            sys_oid_list = list(_SYS_OIDS.values())
            sys_raw = await snmp.get(sys_oid_list)

            # Map OID results back to friendly names
            oid_to_key = {v: k for k, v in _SYS_OIDS.items()}
            sys_data = {}
            for oid, value in sys_raw.items():
                key = oid_to_key.get(oid)
                if key:
                    sys_data[key] = str(value)

            # Build config_data dict
            config_data = {}

            # Stratum (system-level OID)
            config_data["ntp_stratum"] = sys_data.get("ntp_stratum", "16")

            # -- Collect peer table ------------------------------------
            peers = {}  # assoc_id -> {field: value}

            for field_name, base_oid in _PEER_OIDS.items():
                rows = await snmp.walk(base_oid)
                for oid_str, value in rows:
                    # Extract association index from OID suffix
                    suffix = oid_str[len(base_oid) + 1:]
                    if suffix not in peers:
                        peers[suffix] = {}
                    peers[suffix][field_name] = str(value)

            # Add peer data as flat config keys
            peer_count = 0
            reachable_count = 0
            unreachable_count = 0

            for idx, (assoc_id, peer_data) in enumerate(sorted(peers.items())):
                peer_count += 1
                prefix = f"peer_{idx}"

                config_data[f"{prefix}_address"] = peer_data.get("address", "unknown")
                config_data[f"{prefix}_stratum"] = peer_data.get("stratum", "16")

                mode_raw = peer_data.get("mode", "0")
                config_data[f"{prefix}_mode"] = _MODE_MAP.get(mode_raw, f"unknown({mode_raw})")

                reach_raw = peer_data.get("reach", "0")
                try:
                    reach_int = int(reach_raw)
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    reach_int = 0
                is_reachable = reach_int > 0
                config_data[f"{prefix}_reachable"] = "yes" if is_reachable else "no"

                if is_reachable:
                    reachable_count += 1
                else:
                    unreachable_count += 1

                configured = peer_data.get("configured", "2")
                config_data[f"{prefix}_configured"] = "yes" if configured == "1" else "no"

            config_data["ntp_peer_count"] = str(peer_count)
            config_data["ntp_peers_reachable"] = str(reachable_count)
            config_data["ntp_peers_unreachable"] = str(unreachable_count)

            # Derive overall sync status from peer reachability.
            # The previous approach of reading cntpSysSrvStatus (.1.1.11.0) was
            # unreliable — the OID is not implemented on several IOS versions and
            # always returned "unknown". Peer counts are the authoritative signal.
            if peer_count == 0:
                config_data["ntp_sync_status"] = "unknown"
            elif unreachable_count == 0:
                config_data["ntp_sync_status"] = "healthy"
            elif reachable_count == 0:
                config_data["ntp_sync_status"] = "critical"
            else:
                config_data["ntp_sync_status"] = "warning"

            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)

            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=config_data,
                status="ok",
                reachable=True,
                error_message=None,
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
            )

        except Exception as exc:
            execution_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)
            return PollResult(
                job_id=context.job.job_id,
                device_id=context.job.device_id,
                collector_node=context.node_id,
                timestamp=time.time(),
                metrics=[],
                config_data=None,
                status="critical",
                reachable=False,
                error_message=f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}",
                execution_time_ms=execution_ms,
                error_category="device",
            )

display_template.html:
{{ntp_sync_status}} Stratum {{ntp_stratum}}
{{ntp_peers_reachable}} reachable {{ntp_peers_unreachable}} unreachable
Peers {{ntp_peer_count}}
ADDRESS STRATUM MODE REACHABLE CONFIGURED
No NTP peers reported.

alert_definitions.name: Cisco NTP All Peers Unreachable
alert_definitions.description: All configured NTP peers on the device report reach = 0. The device is not synchronizing with any upstream time source and its clock will drift. Check reachability to the NTP servers and ACLs between the device and its peers.

alert_definitions.name: Cisco NTP Partial Peer Loss
alert_definitions.description: At least one configured NTP peer is unreachable but the device is still synchronized with one or more peers. Investigate the affected peer and restore full redundancy.
